segments.text: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; his love endures forever.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Let Israel say: 'His love endures forever.'
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: Let the house of Aaron say: 'His love endures forever.'
segments.speaker: House of Aaron

segments.text: Let those who fear the LORD say: 'His love endures forever.'
segments.speaker: Those who fear the LORD

segments.text: When hard pressed, I cried to the LORD; he brought me into a spacious place.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

footnotes: in distress: Heb. out of distress

segments.text: The LORD is with me; I will not be afraid. What can mere mortals do to me?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: The LORD is with me as my helper. I look in triumph on my enemies.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in humans.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in princes.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: All the nations surrounded me, but in the name of the LORD I cut them down.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: They surrounded me on every side, but in the name of the LORD I cut them down.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: They swarmed around me like bees, but they were consumed as quickly as burning thorns; in the name of the LORD I cut them down.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: I was pushed back and about to fall, but the LORD helped me.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: The LORD is my strength and my defense; he has become my salvation.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Shouts of joy and victory resound in the tents of the righteous: 'The LORD's right hand has done mighty things!'
segments.speaker: Righteous People

segments.text: The LORD's right hand is lifted high; the LORD's right hand has done mighty things!
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: I will not die but live, and will proclaim what the LORD has done.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: The LORD has chastened me severely, but he has not given me over to death.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Open for me the gates of the righteous; I will enter and give thanks to the LORD.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: This is the gate of the LORD through which the righteous may enter.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: I will give you thanks, for you answered me; you have become my salvation.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: the LORD has done this, and it is marvelous in our eyes.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: the LORD's…: Heb. from the LORD

segments.text: The LORD has done it this very day; let us rejoice today and be glad.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: LORD, save us! LORD, grant us success!
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD. From the house of the LORD, we bless you.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: The LORD is God, and he has made his light shine on us. With boughs in hand, join in the festal procession up to the horns of the altar.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: You are my God, and I will praise you; you are my God, and I will exalt you.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; his love endures forever.
segments.speaker: Psalmist
